summary: Validate the systemic M7 interaction recovery before content lock reconsideration
acceptance_criteria: Validate that a player can identify the current phase, the main task, and the next action within the first viewport without opening developer overlays.
Validate that the District 1 troop obligation can be reached and handled from the task surface and selected district context without accidental march/campaign behavior.
Validate that appointment preview and confirmation have visible before/after meaning and unambiguous post-submit result feedback.
Validate that the map, task rail, and inspector stay synchronized across the obligation and appointment paths.
Capture desktop 1440x900 and smaller supported viewport screenshots and check for no unexplained console errors.
Only after independent QA ACCEPT may M7-CONTENT-LOCK-ACCEPTANCE-001 return to product-owner reconsideration; this validation itself does not approve content lock.
required_tests: screenshot evidence for 1440x900 and at least one smaller supported viewport
manual-smoke notes that name what the player can do next and how command result feedback appears
notes: This is a product-owner REQUEST_CHANGES recovery path. It must not approve formal M7 content lock.
The target is a playable, understandable low-fidelity product experience, not high-fidelity final art.
Manual node battle remains DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE and is not reopened by this recovery path.
No server, multiplayer, telemetry, paid service, account, cloud-save implementation, release, branding, or arbitrary-code mod scope is authorized.
UI must use read models and existing command/query boundaries; React, Pixi, and renderer code must not mutate authoritative WorldState.
All player-visible text must pass through existing i18n boundaries.
